response.justification: Candidate is an experienced senior engineer with 10+ years of broad full-stack and systems experience, but C++ exposure is limited to a single 6-month role at National Instruments in 2014-2015. The core of the role (modern C++/STL, multi-threaded networked systems, MQTT, IEC 62443, industrial automation) is essentially absent from the resume. Primary stacks are TypeScript/Node.js/DLang, which do not transfer cleanly to a senior C++ position in industrial automation.
strengths: 10+ years of senior-level software engineering experience across web, embedded, and distributed systems
Strong software craftsmanship background: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, mentoring, and workshop facilitation
Some low-latency, high-throughput backend experience (Dunnhumby/Sociomantic micro-services at millions of req/sec)
concerns: Only ~6 months of professional C++ experience, over a decade ago; no evidence of modern C++/STL proficiency
No demonstrated experience with industrial automation, IEC 62443 SDL, MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus, or related protocols
No cloud (Azure), IoT, or message-broker architecture work explicitly shown that aligns with the role's core stack
